input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
       xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
       xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
       xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
       FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
       Title="MyMauiApp">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, no tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — two sub-tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants a .NET MAUI AppShell.xaml setup with a specific structure. This is a code generation/guidance request - I don't need to explore any codebase. I'll create the files directly.

Let me create:
1. `AppShell.xaml` with flyout structure
2. `AppShell.xaml.cs` with route registration

Structure:
- Flyout with:
  - Dashboard (single page) — FlyoutItem
  - Products (with tabs: Active, Archived) — FlyoutItem with Tab children
  - Settings — FlyoutItem
- Route registration for ProductDetailsPage

data.content:
Here's the structure:

**`AppShell.xaml`** — Three `FlyoutItem`s:
| Flyout entry | Content |
|---|---|
| Dashboard | Single `ShellContent` page |
| Products | A `Tab` with two `ShellContent` children ("Active" / "Archived") — renders as bottom tabs |
| Settings | Single `ShellContent` page |

**`AppShell.xaml.cs`** — Registers `ProductDetailsPage` via `Routing.RegisterRoute` since it's not in the visual tree.

**To navigate** from a product list to details:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
```

You'll still need to create the five `ContentPage` classes under a `Pages/` folder: `DashboardPage`, `ActiveProductsPage`, `ArchivedProductsPage`, `SettingsPage`, and `ProductDetailsPage`. Accept query parameters on the details page with `[QueryProperty("Id", "id")]`.
